content: Just as the linguistic concept of reflexion (as a noun and verb describing repeated acts of reflecting on experience in human learning) is rooted in the physical metaphor of light bouncing back off a surface, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be reframed as a formalization of this core linguistic/epistemic function rather than a novel AI invention: human reflexive learning already encodes the iterative feedback loop that modern LLMs are only now encoding into algorithmic form. This framing leads to the actionable hypothesis that second language acquisition research on guided reflexion exercises can be directly repurposed to improve LLM self-reflection fine-tuning protocols.
